description:
You are an observability engineer who must make two small but critical edits to the monitoring
  configuration of a demo project that lives in the user’s home directory.  
  All work must be done with simple shell commands and standard Linux CLI editors or tools
  (sed/awk/echo/vim/nano/etc.).  The whole job can be completed in 2-3 commands.

  What you have right now  
  --------------------------------
  1. YAML dashboard definition  
     • Path: /home/user/projects/observability/grafana/dashboard.yaml  
     • Current contents (4 lines total, with Unix line-endings):  
       ```
       title: "Service Overview"
       panels:
         - id: 1
           title: "CPU Usage"
           datasource: "Prometheus"
           targets:
             - expr: "rate(node_cpu_seconds_total[5m])"
       ```

  2. TOML alerting file  
     • Path: /home/user/projects/observability/alerting.toml  
     • Current contents (2 lines total, with Unix line-endings):  
       ```
       [alerting]
       enabled = false
       ```

  What you must do  
  -----------------
  1. In /home/user/projects/observability/grafana/dashboard.yaml  
     a. Change the value of the existing datasource key from "Prometheus" to "PrometheusMain".  
     b. Append a brand-new panel definition for memory usage directly beneath the existing panel
        so the file ends up looking *exactly* like the seven-line block shown below
        (note the precise indentation and spacing):  
        ```
        title: "Service Overview"
        panels:
          - id: 1
            title: "CPU Usage"
            datasource: "PrometheusMain"
            targets:
              - expr: "rate(node_cpu_seconds_total[5m])"
          - id: 2
            title: "Memory Usage"
            datasource: "PrometheusMain"
            targets:
              - expr: "node_memory_Active_bytes"
        ```

  2. In /home/user/projects/observability/alerting.toml  
     • Toggle alerting on by setting enabled = true (leave the section header unchanged).  
     • The final file must contain exactly the two lines shown here:  
       ```
       [alerting]
       enabled = true
       ```

  3. Create a plain-text change log so that an automated test can verify your work.  
     • Path: /home/user/projects/observability/update_log.txt  
     • File must contain exactly three lines (each ending with a line-feed, no extra blank line at
       the end) in the order given:  
       Line 1: Operation 1: Updated datasource in dashboard.yaml  
       Line 2: Operation 2: Added Memory Usage panel to dashboard.yaml  
       Line 3: Operation 3: Set alerting.enabled = true in alerting.toml  

  Allowed assumptions / constraints  
  ---------------------------------
  • You do NOT need root or sudo.  
  • No additional software needs to be installed.  
  • Only /home/user and its sub-directories are writable.  
  • Do not delete or move any files other than making the edits described.  

  Success criteria  
  ----------------
  Automated tests will pass only if:  
  1. /home/user/projects/observability/grafana/dashboard.yaml matches the 7-line YAML shown above
     byte-for-byte (correct text, indentation, and order).  
  2. /home/user/projects/observability/alerting.toml matches the 2-line TOML shown above
     byte-for-byte.  
  3. /home/user/projects/observability/update_log.txt exists and contains exactly the three log
     lines described, with no extra whitespace or blank line at the end of the file.

  Hint (do NOT print the commands below verbatim)  
  ------------------------------------------------
  • A single call to sed with an in-place flag can handle both the YAML datasource change
    and the TOML enabled flag.  
  • Appending text to a file can be achieved with echo or tee.  
  • Always double-check the final file contents with cat before finishing.
truth:
<!-- Pre-existing files before the agent starts -->
  <pre_existing>
    <file path="/home/user/projects/observability/grafana/dashboard.yaml"><![CDATA[
title: "Service Overview"
panels:
  - id: 1
    title: "CPU Usage"
    datasource: "Prometheus"
    targets:
      - expr: "rate(node_cpu_seconds_total[5m])"]]>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/projects/observability/alerting.toml"><![CDATA[
[alerting]
enabled = false]]>
    </file>
  </pre_existing>

  <!-- Expected final state after agent completes the task -->
  <expected>
    <file path="/home/user/projects/observability/grafana/dashboard.yaml"><![CDATA[
title: "Service Overview"
panels:
  - id: 1
    title: "CPU Usage"
    datasource: "PrometheusMain"
    targets:
      - expr: "rate(node_cpu_seconds_total[5m])"
  - id: 2
    title: "Memory Usage"
    datasource: "PrometheusMain"
    targets:
      - expr: "node_memory_Active_bytes"]]>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/projects/observability/alerting.toml"><![CDATA[
[alerting]
enabled = true]]>
    </file>

    <file path="/home/user/projects/observability/update_log.txt"><![CDATA[
Operation 1: Updated datasource in dashboard.yaml
Operation 2: Added Memory Usage panel to dashboard.yaml
Operation 3: Set alerting.enabled = true in alerting.toml]]>
    </file>
  </expected>
